name: Calendars — Odd Days and the Day of the Week
whatIsIt: Days of the week repeat every 7 days, so to find which weekday something lands on you only care about the REMAINDER when the number of days is divided by 7 — that remainder is called the ODD DAYS. A normal year has 365 days = 52 weeks + 1 odd day, so the same date shifts forward one weekday next year. A LEAP year has 366 days = 52 weeks + 2 odd days, so it shifts forward two. Count odd days, add them up mod 7, and you know the weekday.
whyNeeded: 'What day was 15 August 1947?' and 'if today is Monday, what day after 100 days?' are classic SSC, Bank, Railway and quiz questions. The odd-days idea is also a neat, real use of remainders (modular arithmetic).
realLifeUses: Finding the weekday of a birthday in a future or past year
Knowing that your birthday shifts one weekday each year (two after a leap year)
Planning: 'what day is 90 days from now?'
SSC / Bank / Railway quantitative aptitude questions

vocabulary.meaning: The remainder of days after taking out whole weeks (days mod 7).
366 days = 52 weeks + 2 odd days (Feb has 29 days).
Only the remainder after dividing by 7 decides the weekday.

story.title: Which Day Is My Birthday Next Year?
story.text: Diya's birthday is on a Wednesday this year. 'What day next year?' Her brother explains: 'A year has 365 days. Take out 52 full weeks (364 days) and 1 day is left over — that's the 1 odd day. So your birthday jumps forward by one weekday, to Thursday. But if a leap year (366 days) falls in between, there are 2 odd days, so it jumps by two.' Diya loves that she only needs the leftover day, never all 365.
story.extractedProblem: If a date is a Wednesday this year, what weekday is the same date next ordinary year?
story.answerInStory: Thursday — an ordinary year has 1 odd day, so the date shifts forward by one weekday.

bars.label: Only the odd days shift the weekday

visual.caption: Weeks repeat, so full weeks change nothing. Only the ODD DAYS (days mod 7) move the weekday forward: +1 per ordinary year, +2 per leap year.

teachingGallery.title: Count odd days, add mod 7
teachingGallery.note: 365 → 1 odd day, 366 → 2 odd days. Any number of days shifts the weekday by (days mod 7).

standardMethod.summary: Reduce the day count mod 7 to get odd days, then move that many weekdays forward.
steps: Count the total days between the two dates.
Odd days = total days ÷ 7, keep the REMAINDER.
Each ordinary year adds 1 odd day; each leap year adds 2.
Move forward by the odd days from the known weekday.

alternateMethods.name: Year-shift shortcut
alternateMethods.whenToUse: Same date, a few years apart.
steps: Add 1 for every ordinary year and 2 for every leap year in between.
Take the total mod 7 and shift the weekday.

workedExamples.problem: How many odd days are in 365 days?
If today is Monday, what day is it after 10 days? (Give the odd days first.)
steps: So it is Thursday; the odd-days count is 3.

trickPractice.intro: Divide the day count by 7 and keep the remainder — that's how many weekdays to jump.

commonMistakes.mistake: Trying to move the weekday by the full number of days instead of the remainder mod 7.
commonMistakes.fix: Full weeks bring you back to the same weekday, so they don't matter. Only the REMAINDER after dividing by 7 (the odd days) shifts the day.

commonMistakes.mistake: Giving a leap year 1 odd day like an ordinary year.
commonMistakes.fix: A leap year has 366 days = 52 weeks + 2, so it has 2 odd days. The same date jumps TWO weekdays across a leap year, not one.

easy.q: An ordinary year (365 days) has how many odd days?

medium.q: If today is Sunday, 7 days later it is…

challenge.q: A leap year has how many odd days?

questions.q: To find a weekday after many days, you use…

teachBackPrompt: Teach a friend odd days: divide the number of days by 7 and keep the remainder. Show that 365 days = 1 odd day (birthday shifts one weekday) and a leap year = 2 odd days, and explain why full weeks don't matter.

revisionCard.summary: Odd days = days mod 7 (remainder after whole weeks). Ordinary year = 1 odd day; leap year = 2 odd days. Add up odd days and shift the weekday forward. Full weeks never change the day — only the remainder does.

gameMission.title: Day-of-the-Week Detective
gameMission.brief: Crack the weekday of any date! Strip out whole weeks, count the odd days, add 1 for ordinary years and 2 for leap years, and land on the right day.

realLifeProject: Find the weekday of your birthday this year, then predict next year's weekday using odd days (1 shift, or 2 if a leap year falls between). Check it on a calendar app.

rememberIt.unpack: Ordinary year +1, leap year +2. Full weeks don't matter — only the remainder shifts the weekday.

formulas.name: Odd days
formulas.whenToUse: Finding a weekday after N days.

studentQuestions.q: Why does my birthday move forward each year?
studentQuestions.a: A year is 365 days = 52 whole weeks plus 1 extra day, so the date lands one weekday later (two later if a leap year is in between).
Every 7 days brings you back to the same weekday, so full weeks cancel out — only the leftover days (mod 7) change the day.
